name: Barion Brown
headline: Track-speed returner buried on a crowded Saints depth chart
body:
Brown arrives in New Orleans as a sixth-round flier whose calling card is raw, vertical speed and a special-teams resume that includes six collegiate kick-return touchdowns. The Saints already roster an established group of pass catchers, so the rookie projects as a depth option fighting for snaps rather than a target earner. The film flags are real: a 36.7% contested-catch rate, drop rates above 12% in two college seasons, and a usage chart where 54.2% of his 2025 looks came within nine yards of the line. That is a limited route tree masking limited current value.

Draft cost matches the profile, with an ADP near pick 347 and a WR201 expert ranking that keeps him off standard-league radars entirely. His realistic 2026 path to relevance runs through the return game, which makes him a return-yardage and deep-dynasty consideration only. Treat him as a speculative stash, not a contributor you start.
